title: Luffy’s Grief and the ASL Past: Rising Conflict
description: In the Post-War / ASL Flashback storyline, this section centers on Sabo, Whitebeard, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #9, Unknown person #14, Unknown person #8 as the scene develops across pages 6–10.
scene: This scene advances the Post-War / ASL Flashback conflict through Sabo, Whitebeard, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #9, Unknown person #14, focusing on the immediate stakes of luffy’s grief and the asl past: rising conflict.

dialogue.speaker: Sabo
dialogue.text: No, no, forget that last part....!!

dialogue.speaker: Sabo
dialogue.text: Hey!!! WH... where are you going ?!!

dialogue.speaker: Sabo
dialogue.text: Sabo-kun, are you listening to me?

dialogue.speaker: Sabo
dialogue.text: SABO is nowhere to be found?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: A fire at the trash heap tonight?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #13
dialogue.text: Good to see you again, little ones...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: The big job goes down tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #14
dialogue.text: I'll tell you all the details later.

summary: This scene advances the Post-War / ASL Flashback conflict through Sabo, Whitebeard, Unknown person #5, Unknown person #9, Unknown person #14, focusing on the immediate stakes of luffy’s grief and the asl past: rising conflict.
